_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to adjust to and operate in a diverse, unpredictable, challenging, and fast-paced work environment.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing data and AI training programs for diverse organizational audiences, from executives to operational staff, with role-appropriate content and delivery formats.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-S-007 (LS, cosine: 0.402), LS-A-001 (LS, cosine: 0.399).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.4/10 (raw 0.241). Shared vocabulary: [diverse]. DCWF-only terms [ability, adjust, challenging, environment, fast, operate] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, appropriate, audiences, content, data, delivery] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.874 (P=0.885, R=0.863). P exceeds R by 0.022 — the DCWF element's 15 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Ability to adjust to and operate in a diverse, unpredictable, challenging, and f" and "Skill in designing data and AI training programs for diverse organizational audi" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.433 > STS 0.241 (gap +0.192). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [diverse] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.1%. Shared tokens: [diverse]. DCWF-only: [ability, adjust, challenging, environment, fast, operate]. WIDAI-only: [ai, appropriate, audiences, content, data, delivery].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.192 with low STS 0.241. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
